title: Galarian Slowbro Battle Guide
summary: A [Poison]/[Psychic] bulky pivot with [regenerator] and signature [shell-side-arm] — restores 1/3 HP on switch and auto-picks the better attacking stat.

recommendedMoves.reason: Signature move — picks the stronger split

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Galarian Slowbro is a bulky pivot defined by [regenerator] — every time it switches out, it recovers 1/3 HP, enabling an infinite-use defensive role. Its signature move [shell-side-arm] automatically picks whichever of its 100 Attack or 100 Special Attack targets the lower defense, making damage output consistently high without mixed investment.

## Strengths
[regenerator] is a top-tier ability in doubles. Combined with [slack-off], Galarian Slowbro maintains HP indefinitely. [shell-side-arm] removes the choice between physical and special output — it picks the better one automatically. The [Poison]/[Psychic] typing resists [Fighting] and gains [Fairy] super-effective coverage.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Ground], [Ghost], [Psychic], and [Dark] are all common super-effective hits. Speed 30 locks it into [trick-room] or slow-pivot usage. [Steel] types like [heatran] wall the set completely.

## Recommended Moveset
[shell-side-arm] is mandatory — it's the whole identity of the set. [psychic] is the secondary STAB. [slack-off] sustains HP between pivots. [protect] is standard doubles utility.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[black-sludge] is ideal for passive recovery on a [Poison] type. [assault-vest] converts it into a special wall. [regenerator] is locked. Tera [Poison] grants a [Psychic] resistance, Tera [Psychic] stacks STAB on [psychic].

## Teammates & Synergy
Fits on slow pivot cores with [intimidate] support and [trick-room] setters. Cover [Ground] and [Ghost] with [Flying] and [Dark] partners. The [regenerator] core means Galarian Slowbro can absorb status and still heal back.

## Play Patterns
Switch in on a predicted resisted hit, click [shell-side-arm] or [psychic], then pivot out to trigger [regenerator]. Use [slack-off] when HP drops and a switch isn't safe. Against [Steel] walls, pivot out immediately.
